decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo pieces needed to win the game using The Fantasticar and Thassa's Oracle. The deck aims to use efficient cantrips like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Mishra's Bauble to set up the Doomsday combo. It also includes disruption in the form of Thoughtseize and Daze to protect the combo from opponent's interference. Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual help accelerate the deck's game plan, making it a fast and resilient combo deck in the Legacy format.

Utilize disruptive spells and card advantage engines to control the game, protect key pieces, and out-value opponents in order to win through attrition. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan with counterspells and removal, while also generating card advantage through card draw and recursion with cards like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, and Quantum Riddler. By efficiently managing resources and keeping the opponent off balance, the deck can outlast the opponent and ultimately come out ahead in the long game.

This control deck focuses on leveraging Leyline of the Guildpact to bolster its spells, while disrupting opponents with counterspells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce. It seeks to maintain board control with removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and prevent opponents from executing their game plan by utilizing cards like Lavinia, Azorius Renegade. The deck also aims to generate card advantage and manipulate its draws with Brainstorm and Ponder. Through a combination of disruption, card advantage, and efficient threats like Scion of Draco, this deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver its opponents in Legacy 1 vs 1 matches.

The main idea of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's resources while building up your own advantage through sacrificing lands, creatures, and artifacts. By using cards like Smallpox, Thoughtseize, and Malevolent Rumble, you can control the game and limit your opponent's options. With the help of cards like Life from the Loam and Urza's Saga, you can generate value and set up powerful plays. This deck aims to grind out the opponent and outlast them in a war of attrition, ultimately taking control of the game and securing victory.

The main concept of this deck is to efficiently ramp up mana using cards like Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb, while utilizing control elements such as Force of Will and Veil of Summer to protect key combo pieces like Aluren and Show and Tell. The deck also includes a strong tutor package with cards like Brainstorm and Ponder to find the necessary combo pieces. Acererak the Archlich and Atraxa, Grand Unifier are used as win conditions, along with the ability to quickly cast spells like Omniscience for a powerful board presence.

The deck focuses on utilizing a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells to put pressure on the opponent while also disrupting their game plan. By combining powerful creatures like Stoneforge Mystic and Skyclave Apparition with cards like Swords to Plowshares and Thoughtseize, the deck aims to control the board and force the opponent into unfavorable situations. With the help of its companion, Yorion, Sky Nomad, the deck can maximize its value and overwhelm the opponent with a combination of threats and disruption.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through a combination of land destruction, disruptive spells, and powerful threats. The key strategy is to use cards like Sphere of Resistance and Wasteland to slow down opponents, while setting up powerful land combos like Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage. Life from the Loam helps ensure a steady stream of lands while Malevolent Rumble can clear the board of threats. The deck aims to disrupt opponents' game plans and overwhelm them with a combination of control elements and powerful land interactions.

The deck revolves around assembling a powerful combo involving The Fantasticar, The One Ring, and Manifold Key to generate immense value and overwhelming amounts of mana to cast devastating spells like Kozilek's Command and Tezzeret, Cruel Captain. The deck also utilizes artifact ramp like Grim Monolith and Lotus Petal, along with disruptive elements like Chalice of the Void and Pithing Needle to control the game. The mana base is optimized for fast and consistent mana production, allowing the deck to quickly set up its game-winning combos.

This deck focuses on quickly assembling a board presence with artifact creatures and using Urza's Saga to create powerful Artifact tokens. Emry, Lurker of the Loch serves as a way to recur artifacts from the graveyard, while Pinnacle Emissary and Kappa Cannoneer provide aggressive threats. The deck also utilizes disruptive elements like Force of Will and Pithing Needle to control the game. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells in a Legacy 1 vs 1 format.

The main idea of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while fast-tracking your own victory. By using a combination of disruptive spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will, along with removal like Fatal Push, the deck aims to control the board and protect its threats. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provides card advantage, while Murktide Regent and Barrowgoyf serve as powerful, efficient beaters. The deck also includes versatile spells like Brainstorm and Ponder to dig for necessary cards, as well as tempo plays like Daze to disrupt the opponent's tempo.

The deck focuses on assembling the combo of Doomsday and Thassa's Oracle to win the game in one explosive turn. It utilizes cards like Force of Will, Daze, and Thoughtseize to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent. The deck includes cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder to help find key pieces quickly, along with fast mana like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual to speed up the combo. Street Wraith and Flow State provide additional ways to dig through the deck for combo pieces, while also serving as alternate win conditions. The deck is designed to quickly and efficiently win the game through a well-executed combo.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana and filling the graveyard to enable casting Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis as early as possible. Utilizing cards like Stitcher's Supplier and Wight of the Reliquary to fill the graveyard, cards like Sylvan Safekeeper to protect key pieces, and disruption cards like Thoughtseize to disrupt the opponent's game plan, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and efficient removal spells. The combination of graveyard synergy and disruption creates a powerful combo strategy that can quickly close out games.

This deck aims to control the game through a combination of resource denial and land manipulation. Using cards like Sphere of Resistance and Crop Rotation, the deck slows down the opponent while quickly ramping up its own mana with Exploration and Mox Diamond. The key win condition is through the Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage combo, creating a 20/20 Marit Lage token to quickly finish off the opponent. The deck also utilizes Urza's Saga to search for key artifacts like Pithing Needle and Expedition Map to further disrupt the opponent's strategy.

The deck focuses on using artifacts to generate mana and control the board, while utilizing combo pieces like The Fantasticar and Mystic Forge to create explosive combos. The key strategy is to quickly assemble the necessary pieces to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory in a Legacy 1 vs 1 format. By utilizing powerful artifacts and mana acceleration, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and establish dominance on the battlefield.

Utilizing a mix of counterspells, removal, and card advantage, this control deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while setting up its own win condition. Through cards like Brainstorm, Force of Will, and Stifle, the deck seeks to control the board and prevent the opponent from executing their strategies. With access to powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo and Teferi, the deck can generate card advantage and provide additional disruption. By managing resources efficiently and utilizing its versatile card pool, the deck looks to outplay and outmaneuver opponents in order to secure victory.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by leveraging powerful counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage to disrupt opponents while also generating card advantage through card draw and filtering effects. The deck aims to stabilize the board and outvalue opponents with powerful threats like Murktide Regent and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, while also having access to efficient removal in Swords to Plowshares. The mana base is designed to support a diverse range of spells with a mix of fetch lands and dual lands.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize Aluren to cheat huge creatures like Acererak the Archlich and Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play for free through combos like Show and Tell, while also using cards like Lotus Petal and Stock Up to generate mana and draw cards. The deck aims to quickly establish a dominant board presence and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats. Cards like Force of Will and Veil of Summer provide protection and disruption to ensure the combo can be executed smoothly.

This deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with efficient creatures and spells. By using cards like Thoughtseize, Force of Will, and Daze to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while deploying threats like Murktide Regent and Barrowgoyf, the deck aims to control the tempo of the game and close out matches quickly. The deck also utilizes powerful card selection and manipulation with cards like Brainstorm and Ponder to ensure a consistent game plan. Overall, the deck focuses on applying pressure while disrupting the opponent's plans to secure victory.

This Dimir Tempo deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's game plan while maintaining a consistent and aggressive tempo. With a mix of powerful disruption spells like Force of Will and Thoughtseize, along with efficient removal like Fatal Push, the deck aims to control the game and maintain board presence. The deck also utilizes efficient threats like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Murktide Regent to close out the game quickly. Overall, the strategy revolves around disrupting the opponent's plays, while applying pressure and finishing the game with powerful threats.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly establish board control and disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure with aggressive creatures. This is achieved through a combination of efficient removal spells like Fatal Push, disruption tools like Thoughtseize, and tempo plays like Daze. The deck also aims to maintain card advantage through cards like Brainstorm and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. By efficiently managing resources and disrupting the opponent's strategy, this Aggro-Dimir Tempo deck aims to outpace and outmaneuver the opponent to secure a victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing cheap disruptive spells and efficient creatures to pressure opponents while controlling the board. It aims to disrupt opponents' game plans with cards like Thoughtseize, Force of Will, and Daze, while deploying threats like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Nethergoyf to quickly close out the game. By combining aggression with tempo control, the deck seeks to create a fast-paced and interactive playstyle that keeps opponents on their toes. The inclusion of versatile spells like Brainstorm and Fatal Push further enhances the deck's flexibility and adaptability to various matchups.

This Eldrazi aggro deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures like Reality Smasher and Thought-Knot Seer while disrupting their game plan with Chalice of the Void and Thorn of Amethyst. The deck utilizes mana acceleration from cards like Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb to play its threats ahead of schedule. With the ability to lock down the opponent's spells and lands, along with the aggressive nature of its creatures, this deck aims to quickly close out games before the opponent can stabilize.

The deck aims to utilize cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch, Goblin Engineer, and Goblin Welder to manipulate artifacts and create powerful combos. By assembling key pieces like Sewer-veillance Cam, Urza's Bauble, and Mishra's Bauble, the deck can generate significant value and disrupt opponent's strategies. The inclusion of cards like Pinnacle Emissary and Phyrexian Dragon Engine allows for alternate win conditions or additional utility. Urza's Saga plays a crucial role in tutoring for necessary artifacts, while the mana base provides consistency and versatility. Overall, the deck thrives on synergy and resourceful plays to outmaneuver opponents.

The deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells, using cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Ocelot Pride to deal damage efficiently. It also includes removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and disruption cards like Thoughtseize and Cabal Therapy to disrupt opponents' strategies. The deck also utilizes cards like Goblin Bombardment for additional reach and pressure. The mana base is optimized for consistency and includes powerful lands like Karakas and Wasteland to control the opponent's board. Overall, the deck focuses on proactive aggression and disruption to quickly end games.

The deck aims to create a powerful combo using Painter's Servant and Grindstone to mill the opponent's library. It also utilizes Goblin Welder and Goblin Engineer to tutor and recur important artifacts. The deck includes disruption with Red Elemental Blast and Pyroblast, as well as mana acceleration with Moxen and Ancient Tomb. Additionally, it has a variety of artifacts to benefit from Painter's Servant's ability, such as Mox Opal and Soul-Guide Lantern. The deck focuses on assembling the combo quickly while controlling the opponent's resources to secure the win.

This deck focuses on disrupting and controlling the opponent through resource denial, hand disruption, and removal spells while generating value through repeatable token creation and land recursion. It aims to create a lock on the game by utilizing cards like Smallpox and Malevolent Rumble to strip the opponent of resources while deploying threats like Barrowgoyf and Hogaak, Arisen Necropolis. The deck also utilizes Urza's Saga to tutor for important artifacts and lands, further enhancing its control elements and overwhelming the opponent with a combination of disruption and threats.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Thassa's Oracle and Doomsday to win the game in one powerful turn. The key strategy involves using cards like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Thoughtseize to set up the perfect pile of cards with Doomsday, while protecting the combo with Force of Will and Daze. The Fantasticar serves as a versatile utility card, providing additional card draw and mana acceleration. The deck also includes disruption with cards like Thoughtseize and Flow State to disrupt the opponent's game plan, allowing for a smooth execution of the combo.

This deck revolves around using the combo of Doomsday and Thassa's Oracle to win the game quickly. The Fantasticar helps to set up the combo by digging through the deck for the necessary pieces, while also providing protection with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze. The deck aims to be fast and efficient, using cards like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual to accelerate its game plan. Thoughtseize and other disruptive spells help to control the opponent's game plan, allowing the combo to go off smoothly.

This combo deck aims to efficiently assemble and utilize The Fantasticar along with cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to generate mana quickly and cast a game-winning Doomsday spell. The deck also includes interaction and protection in the form of Force of Will, Daze, and Thoughtseize. Thassa's Oracle serves as the win condition once the necessary cards are put into place. The deck focuses on careful planning and sequencing of spells to execute its game plan effectively, while also being able to adapt to different situations with cards like Brainstorm and Ponder.

The main focus of this deck is to utilize The Fantasticar as a combo piece, along with Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Thought Monitor to generate card advantage and mana acceleration. Chalice of the Void is used to disrupt opponent's strategies, while Urza's Saga helps to tutor key artifacts. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm opponents with a powerful board presence. This deck is designed to be fast and efficient, with the ability to adapt to different matchups and control the game through various interactions.

This deck focuses on deploying threats quickly and disrupting opponent's plans with counterspells and removal. It utilizes efficient creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Delver of Secrets to pressure the opponent while protecting them with cards like Force of Will and Daze. The deck also includes tempo plays like Mishra's Bauble and Cori-Steel Cutter to maintain card advantage and disrupt the opponent's game plan. Overall, the deck aims to establish an early advantage and close out the game before the opponent can stabilize.

The main goal of this UWx Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining card advantage and control of the game. It focuses on using efficient removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and countermagic like Force of Will to stop key threats. The deck also utilizes card selection and manipulation through Brainstorm, Ponder, and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student to find answers and maintain consistency. With a mix of powerful planeswalkers like Teferi, Time Raveler and efficient threats like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd, the deck aims to outmaneuver and outvalue the opponent in a resource management battle.
